title: Build checks: citation-coverage + readability + "not medical advice" label lint + staleness
context: Ewing-Family-Guide is a free, plain-language guide for families, caregivers, and AYA patients facing a Ewing sarcoma diagnosis. It ONLY restates and organizes vetted-authority information (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG), with a citation on every assertion, at a health-literacy reading level. This is HIGH risk-tier, patient-facing health content: nothing patient-facing ships until both a credentialed pediatric/sarcoma oncologist AND a patient/caregiver advocate sign off (version-scoped, either can block). No delivery partner, oncologist reviewer, or patient-advocate reviewer is yet secured. This task scaffolds the lightweight TypeScript/ESM build checks (per Elyos conventions) that make the provenance and labeling rules enforceable in CI. It is infra/tooling, not patient-facing content. Binding cancer guardrails apply: open-access/aggregate/de-identified educational sources only; controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks) and any identifiable patient data are out of scope; verify each source license; provenance on every assertion; no medical advice, diagnosis, or individual prognosis.
objective: Implement, as a PR, the four automated build checks over the content repo: citation-coverage, readability, 'not medical advice' label lint, and source staleness.
acceptanceCriteria: Citation-coverage check fails the build if any assertion lacks >=1 vetted Source
Readability check fails a page that exceeds the grade bar (target <= grade 8)
Label lint fails a page missing the standing 'not medical advice' framing
Staleness check flags/withholds any page whose sources are past validUntil
Checks are TypeScript/ESM per Elyos conventions; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; CI green
